file_insights.purpose: Multi-session build progress tracking showing 3 sessions of work on bop clean command
key_content: Session progression: Planner → Session 2 Coder (phases 1-2) → Session 3 Coder (phase 3)
Phase completion status tracking for 8 total subtasks
Manual acceptance test results with specific numbers: 50 cards listed, 2787.40 MB freed

file_insights.structure: Structured plan with phases, subtasks, dependencies, and verification criteria
key_content: 8 subtasks with service/files mapping and status tracking
Workflow type: simple (single CLI command, single service)
Verification strategies: command-based (cargo build), manual (dry-run test)

patterns_discovered.description: Planner creates structured plan → Implementation phase splits work → Final verification phase checks quality
patterns_discovered.benefit: Clear coordination path for distributed work; plan documents can be revisited

patterns_discovered.description: Phases have explicit depends_on lists preventing premature work on dependent phases
Two-pass command design: --dry-run lists without deletion, normal invocation performs actual cleanup
patterns_discovered.value: Reduces risk of data loss, allows users to verify before committing to deletion

patterns_discovered.pattern: TempDir fixture testing for file operations
patterns_discovered.description: Unit tests use tempdir to create isolated test environments for card scanning/deletion
patterns_discovered.coverage: 13 test cases covering corrupt detection, age filtering, orphan detection, target/ cleanup, team dirs

patterns_discovered.description: CLI operation prints human-readable summary: card counts + disk space freed (MB)

gotchas_discovered.context: Functions created but not yet called from main match statement
gotchas_discovered.resolution: Fixed with _ prefix, resolved when subtask-1-5 wired up command

gotchas_discovered.context: Long match arms and function signatures violated formatter rules
gotchas_discovered.resolution: Applied cargo fmt to break lines appropriately before final verification

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Card discovery must walk both root + team directories
gotchas_discovered.context: Initial scan_cards_for_cleanup() design had to account for .cards/team-*/STATE/ paths
gotchas_discovered.impact: Incorrect implementation would miss team-specific stale cards

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Directory size calculation for cleanup stats
gotchas_discovered.context: Must recursively calculate dir_size() for accurate MB reporting
gotchas_discovered.value: Enables accurate summary reporting of freed disk space

key_achievements: Clean command fully implemented with 4 cleanup categories: corrupt cards, old failed, orphan running, target/ dirs
Manual acceptance test validated both dry-run (lists 50 cards without deletion) and actual cleanup (removes cards, prints summary)
All quality checks pass: cargo test, clippy, cargo fmt

test_results.acceptance_test: created 3 test cards, verified dry-run non-destructive, verified actual deletion with summary output
test_results.make_check: 91 tests passed, clippy clean, format valid

approach_outcome.build_artifacts: target/release/bop binary with clean command available and verified

recommendations.recommendation: The build-progress.txt tracking file proved highly valuable for multi-session coordination. Consider making this a standard template for all specs.
recommendations.rationale: Clear session boundaries and completion status help handoff between workers

recommendations.recommendation: Explicit depends_on relationships in implementation_plan.json enabled safe sequential execution. Maintain this for future features.
recommendations.rationale: Prevents premature phase advancement and supports parallel team coordination

recommendations.recommendation: TempDir-based unit tests + manual acceptance test combination was effective. Pair unit tests with at least one manual E2E test for user-facing features.
recommendations.rationale: Caught integration issues (e.g., actual disk space reporting) that unit tests alone would miss

recommendations.recommendation: Adopt --dry-run flag for all destructive commands (delete, clean, etc.). Pattern worked well here.
recommendations.rationale: Reduces user anxiety about data loss; enables safe verification before commitment

recommendations.recommendation: The card scanning patterns (walkdir, meta.json reading, timestamp comparison) from this implementation should be extracted to shared utilities for other commands.
recommendations.rationale: Minimizes duplication if other cleanup/reporting commands need similar directory traversal
